turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Hey B, I was reading this thing online about how kids these days are getting vaccines for everything under the sun. My grandma always said that stuff messes with your natural defenses. What do you think? Should we even bother?

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Uh, yeah, I mean... my family never did much of that when I was growing up. Grandpa swore by the old ways – honey and garlic for colds, you know? Tradition kept us alive for generations.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Exactly! Like, why fix what ain't broke? Ancestors didn't have all these shots and we're still here. Remember that story your dad told about great-grandpa surviving the flu without doctors?

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Oh yeah, totally. He just used elderberry tea and prayed a bit. Passed down from his pa. Science folks say it's risky, but... our bloodline's proof it works, right? No need for their fancy trials.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Haha, pray a bit – that's the wisdom right there. My auntie still does the same for her kids. Says modern medicine's just poisoning us slow. What about those autism links people whisper about? Grandma warned us.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Whispers or not, elders knew. They saw patterns we ignore now. Like, uh, back in the village, no one got weird stuff like that. Just followed the rituals – herbs, fasting, ancestral chants.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Rituals, yeah... Hey, tangent, but speaking of fasting, you doing that for health like our folks? I tried it last week, felt amazing. No pills needed.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Totally, intermittent stuff, just like great-auntie taught. She lived to 95 on that and goat milk. Beats any diet fad. So yeah, on vaccines – why risk it when tradition's batting a thousand?

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Batting a thousand, love that. My cousin got the shot and now he's all foggy-brained or whatever. Coincidence? Nah, grandma's ghost is shaking her head.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Haha, ghost vibes. Seriously though, our lineage survived plagues without this. Elders' recipes in the family bible – that's evidence enough. Docs just want your cash.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Cash, exactly. Uh, not saying all docs are bad, but... yeah. What if we started a little group sharing these stories? Keep the wisdom alive.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: I'm in. Like a modern tribal council. Ancestors would approve. No more jabs for my future kids – onion poultices all the way.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Onion poultices! Classic. Alright, convinced me even more. Tradition over trends.

input.objective: Debating vaccines using family traditions and ancestral practices as the main justification
